name: Uno Flip
avoid_if: kids are very tired or attention is very low
why: Fast turns and flip mechanic keep everyone on edge — adults and kids equally surprised

name: Sushi Go!
avoid_if: very young kids (under 7) without adult help
why: Drafting mechanic is simple to learn but adults genuinely strategize — beautiful cards kids love too

name: Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza
avoid_if: kids are tired or easily frustrated
why: Ridiculous name, instant chaos, zero learning curve — best when kids have energy to burn

name: Connect Four
avoid_if: more than 2 players — others waiting around gets boring fast
why: Head-to-head, fast, satisfying — surprisingly strategic once kids hit 7+

name: Jenga
avoid_if: very young kids or extremely short patience — one bad pull kills the mood
why: Builds real tension across all ages — adults and kids equally nervous on hard pulls

name: Dobble / Spot It!
avoid_if: kids under 6 or anyone who dislikes speed pressure
why: Pure reflexes — adults don't automatically beat kids, which makes it genuinely fair

name: Exploding Kittens
avoid_if: very young kids who can't handle losing with humor
why: Silly art, sneaky strategy, light backstabbing — adults enjoy it as much as kids do

name: Sleeping Queens
avoid_if: kids under 5 without adult help — too many card types to remember
why: Gentle strategy that sneaks in basic math — adults enjoy the surprise combo plays

name: Skip-Bo
avoid_if: short sessions — needs 30+ min to feel satisfying, not a quick game
why: Relaxed sequential card play — good for calm evenings when you want something longer

name: King of Tokyo
avoid_if: kids under 7 or groups that get upset with direct conflict
why: Monster battle with dice and cards — one of the rare games where adults are genuinely excited too

name: Charades
avoid_if: shy kids or adults who hate performing — kills the game instantly
why: No materials, instant laughter, adults often lose to kids — natural equalizer

name: 20 Questions
avoid_if: very young kids (under 6) who need too many hints to keep it fun
why: Zero setup, works anywhere — perfect for restaurants or car trips

name: Go Fish
avoid_if: anyone over age 7 who's played it before — low engagement for adults
why: Works with very young kids (4+), teaches memory basics — honest: low adult enjoyment

name: Blink
avoid_if: more than 2 players or anyone who dislikes fast-paced head-to-head competition
why: World's fastest card game — pure reflex and pattern recognition, rounds over in 2 minutes

name: Story Chain
avoid_if: kids too tired to be creative — they'll just say silly random words
why: Each person adds one sentence to a story — hilarious results, zero materials, works anywhere
